author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
Predestination is Sometimes Signified Under the Name of Foreknowledge.
originalBook: A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
title: Predestination is Sometimes Signified Under the Name of Foreknowledge.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: These gifts, therefore, of God, which are given to the elect who are called according to God’s purpose, among which gifts is both the beginning of belief and perseverance in the faith to the termination of this life, as I have proved by such a concurrent testimony of reasons and authorities,—these gifts of God, I say, if there is no such predestination as I am maintaining, are not foreknown by God.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But they are foreknown.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: This, therefore, is the predestination which I maintain.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Consequently sometimes the same predestination is signified also under the name of foreknowledge; as says the apostle, “God has not rejected His people whom He foreknew.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Here, when he says, “He foreknew,” the sense is not rightly understood except as “He predestinated,” as is shown by the context of the passage itself.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For he was speaking of the remnant of the Jews which were saved, while the rest perished.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For above he had said that the prophet had declared to Israel, “All day long I have stretched forth my hands to an unbelieving and a gainsaying people.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And as if it were answered, What, then, has become of the promises of God to Israel? he added in continuation, “I say, then, has God cast away His people?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: God forbid! for I also am an Israelite, of the seed of Abraham, of the tribe of Benjamin.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Then he added the words which I am now treating: “God hath not cast away His people whom He foreknew.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And in order to show that the remnant had been left by God’s grace, not by any merits of their works, he went on to add, “Know ye not what the Scripture saith in Elias, in what way he maketh intercession with God against Israel?” and the rest.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: “But what,” says he, “saith the answer of God unto him?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: ‘I have reserved to myself seven thousand men, who have not bowed the knee before Baal.’”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For He says not, “There are left to me,” or “They have reserved themselves to me,” but, “I have reserved to myself.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: “Even so, then, at this present time also there is made a remnant by the election of grace.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And if of grace, then it is no more by works; otherwise grace is no more grace.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And connecting this with what I have above quoted, “What then?” and in answer to this inquiry, he says, “Israel hath not obtained that which he was seeking for, but the election hath obtained it, and the rest were blinded.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Therefore, in the election, and in this remnant which were made so by the election of grace, he wished to be understood the people which God did not reject, because He foreknew them.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: This is that election by which He elected those, whom He willed, in Christ before the foundation of the world, that they should be holy and without spot in His sight, in love, predestinating them unto the adoption of sons.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: No one, therefore, who understands these things is permitted to doubt that, when the apostle says, “God hath not cast away His people whom He foreknew,” He intended to signify predestination.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For He foreknew the remnant which He should make so according to the election of grace.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: That is, therefore, He predestinated them; for without doubt He foreknew if He predestinated; but to have predestinated is to have foreknown that which He should do.
